content.question: To what extent do inter-municipal competition for enrollment and ethnic segregation dynamics distort simple distance/capacity-based rationalization models in practice, and how should these factors be weighted against geographic efficiency in network planning?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a survey and administrative enrollment analysis across a representative sample of 30–50 Hungarian micro-regions, measuring: pupil flow patterns relative to distance, explicit inter-municipal competition rhetoric in closure/merger debates, and ethnic-demographic composition of source and destination schools; quantify the proportion of enrollment variance explained by distance versus perceived school status and community ethnic composition.
